title: Barleria mutabilis
taxonomy: Barleria mutabilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The Acanthaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or acanthus family. This family consists of approximately 2,964 species, including herbs, shrubs, and trees. Barleria mutabilis is further classified as a member of the genus Barleria, which includes around 400 species of flowering plants. The classification of Barleria mutabilis is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features.
uses: Barleria mutabilis is known for its various medicinal properties, including its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, headache, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to create remedies for these ailments. In some cultures, Barleria mutabilis is used to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Barleria mutabilis' medicinal uses.

parts: Barleria mutabilis, the medicinal parts of this plant are typically used to treat various health issues. preparations: Barleria mutabilis herbal preparations are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Indian Ayurveda and African folk medicine. intro: Barleria mutabilis, also known as "Madagascar Periwinkle," is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Barleria mutabilis include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Barleria mutabilis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, tonics, and ointments for various health applications.
